findings.claim: `.Rbuildignore` already contained `^archive$` before this session, so moving retired vignettes into `archive/retired_vignettes/` excludes them from the built package and from R CMD check with no further config; the move used `git mv` so git records them as renames (history preserved).
findings.source: .Rbuildignore line ^archive$ (already present before the move); archive/retired_vignettes/ holds the 3 retired .Rmd
findings.verification: .Rbuildignore:9 ^archive$ — NOT in commit 64f8fe9 changed-files; added 2026-04-06 (66e6818). Move done via git mv (renames in commit).

findings.claim: ggpaintr declares `Depends: R (>= 4.0.0)` and does NOT re-export `%||%`, so a vignette/example using `%||%` must load rlang (or shiny) explicitly — base R's `%||%` only exists from 4.4.0, so `library(ggpaintr); library(ggplot2)` alone does not guarantee it.
findings.source: DESCRIPTION Depends R(>=4.0.0); NAMESPACE has no %||% reexport; rlang exports %||%; base %||% only since R 4.4.0
findings.verification: DESCRIPTION still `Depends: R (>= 4.0.0)` (lines 38-39), ggplot2 in Depends but NOT rlang. NAMESPACE has `importFrom(rlang,"%||%")` at line 134 (internal IMPORT only) and NO `export("%||%")` — so the package uses %||% internally but does NOT re-export it; `library(ggpaintr); library(ggplot2)` alone does not put %||% on the user search path, and base R's %||% only exists from 4.4.0. The tutorial vignette (the file that drifted) confirms the contract: line 23 `library(rlang)   # for %||%, sym(), call2(), abort() used in the examples`, and %||% is used at lines 117/119/121/172/224/341/343. Claim holds verbatim.

findings.claim: As of 2026-05-30 the intended vignette set is exactly THREE — `ggpaintr-tutorial` (new), `ggpaintr-safety` (rewritten), `ggpaintr-llm` (separate session) — with use-cases/customization/gallery retired to archive; `_pkgdown.yml` articles and all live cross-references were repointed accordingly.
findings.source: vignettes/ now holds ggpaintr-{tutorial,safety,llm}.Rmd; _pkgdown.yml articles = tutorial/safety/llm; archive/retired_vignettes/ = use-cases/customization/gallery
findings.verification: vignettes/ holds exactly ggpaintr-{tutorial,safety,llm}.Rmd; _pkgdown.yml:78-84 articles = tutorial/safety/llm; archive/retired_vignettes/ = use-cases/customization/gallery. Exact match to claim.

findings.claim: A custom placeholder's `build_ui` is never called bare — `invoke_build_ui()` does `do.call(build_ui, c(list(node, label=copy$label), extra_named, extra, ...))`, injecting `label`, `selected` (=node$default), and `named_args` only when the hook's formals name them OR the hook has `...`; this is why the idiomatic signature is `function(node, label=NULL, selected=NULL, ...)` — `...` swallows framework-injected args the hook doesn't consume.
findings.detail: The injector guards each injection with `hook_accepts_dots || "<arg>" %in% fmls`. So a build_ui lacking both `...` and `selected` never receives the boot default. `node$id` arrives already namespaced (rendered_node$id <- ns_fn(node$id)); the hook must use node$id verbatim and must NOT re-namespace.
findings.source: R/paintr-build-ui.R invoke_build_ui (do.call(entry$build_ui, c(list(rendered_node, label=copy$label), extra_named, extra, list(...))))
findings.verification: R/paintr-build-ui.R:824-877 invoke_build_ui — confirmed do.call(build_ui, c(list(rendered_node,label=copy$label),extra_named,extra,...)); rendered_node$id<-ns_fn(node$id); injection guarded by hook_accepts_dots||"selected"/"named_args" %in% fmls (lines 858-873).

findings.claim: A custom source placeholder whose `resolve_data` loads an object by name must capture the registration environment (`env <- environment()`) and `get(nm, envir=env, inherits=TRUE)` — NOT `parent.frame()` at resolve time, because `resolve_data` runs inside the framework's call stack where `parent.frame()` is not the author's script env and would miss user data frames.

The three public ptr_define_placeholder_* constructors are thin wrappers over internal ptr_define_core(role, ...), which validates hook arity at DEFINITION time: build_ui must be function(node,...) for value/source and function(node,cols,data,...) for consumer; value/consumer require resolve_expr + optional validate_session_input(value,ctx); source requires resolve_data(value,node), makes resolve_expr optional (defaulting to rlang::sym(value)), and takes a shortcut logical.
findings.detail: Verified by headless run: all four example registrations (value/consumer/source/ppSize) passed validate_hook and registered. A wrong build_ui arity aborts at the constructor call, not at app launch — useful for vignette examples (they fail fast).
findings.source: R/paintr-registry.R ptr_define_core (validate_hook build_ui_args = c('node','cols','data') for consumer else 'node'); consumer/value require resolve_expr + optional validate_input(value,ctx); source requires resolve_data(value,node) + optional resolve_expr defaulting to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value) + shortcut logical
findings.verification: R/paintr-registry.R ptr_define_core (def at line 355, body 360-424) confirms: build_ui_args = c('node','cols','data') for consumer else 'node' (lines 364-369); source branch (371-383) validates resolve_data c('value','node'), defaults resolve_expr to `function(value, node, ...) rlang::sym(value)` when NULL else validates it, and checks `shortcut` is a single logical; value/consumer branch (384-389) validates resolve_expr and optional validate_session_input c('value','ctx'). ADR-0027 renamed the public constructor arg `validate_input`->`validate_session_input` (formal at 357), but the role-branching + arity-at-definition mechanism the claim describes is intact (internal record key stays `validate_input`, line 413). Claim still true; the constructor-arg name in the claim's phrasing is now `validate_session_input`.

findings.claim: `unsafe_expr_denylist` has EXACTLY 151 entries (confirmed by `length(ggpaintr:::unsafe_expr_denylist)`), and `validate_expr_safety`'s walker caps recursion at depth 100, handling bare symbol / string-literal (each element) / pairlist (recurse) / call (recurse head+args, skip placeholder-annotation calls whose head is in placeholder_names, two-phase compound-head check).
findings.source: R/paintr-utils.R unsafe_expr_denylist (length 151, verified via length() headless); validate_expr_safety walk_expr max_depth=100L; node branches pairlist/character(len1='string literal',>1='character vector')/symbol/call

findings.claim: After retiring the three vignettes, ~14 vignette-app fixtures and the e2e coverage-audit comments are now PROVENANCE-orphaned from any live vignette — their `>>> verbatim:` headers were repointed to `archive/retired_vignettes/...` (still byte-true against the archived chunk) rather than to tutorial chunks, because the new tutorial chunks are different code and a "verbatim" claim against them would be false.
findings.detail: This means the fixtures still test real behavior but no LIVE vignette pairs them; a future pass may want to re-pair the still-relevant ones (consumer-colvars, module-app, single-instance-shared, value-range, source-dataset) to tutorial chunks and drop the gallery-only ones.
findings.source: tests/testthat/fixtures/vignette-apps/*/app.R provenance comments now cite archive/retired_vignettes/*.Rmd; tests/testthat/test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R coverage-audit headers cite archive paths
findings.verification: 14 fixture app.R provenance comments now cite archive/retired_vignettes/*.Rmd; e2e coverage-audit header has 6 archive cites. Fixtures remain runnable app.R (real behavior), but no LIVE vignette pairs them.

findings.claim: `named_args` on a placeholder constructor is a fully-named list mapping extra formula-call argument names to validator functions (same family as `positional_arg`); the name `shared` is explicitly rejected (reserved for cross-widget binding), and the validated values reach `build_ui` as the `named_args` list — letting a formula write e.g. `ppPercent(40, step = 5)`.
findings.source: R/paintr-registry.R validate_named_args (fully-named list of functions; rejects entry named 'shared'); invoke_build_ui injects node$named_args as named_args=
findings.verification: R/paintr-registry.R:226-262 validate_named_args — fully-named list of functions; rejects name 'shared' (250); non-function entries rejected (259). Injected to build_ui as named_args (build-ui.R:870-873).

findings.claim: `ptr_app()`'s only required argument is `formula`; there is no `data=` parameter — data is resolved by looking up bare symbols in `envir` (default `parent.frame()`) or produced by a `ppUpload`/source placeholder.
findings.source: R/paintr-app.R ptr_app signature (formula, envir=parent.frame(), ui_text, expr_check, safe_to_remove, css, spec) — no data= param
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R:134-152 ptr_app(formula, envir=parent.frame(), ui_text=NULL, expr_check=TRUE, safe_to_remove=character(), css=NULL, spec=NULL) — no data= param.

findings.claim: `ptr_shared(formulas)$panel_keys` is exactly the keys referenced by >=2 formulas (verified headless: two iris formulas sharing `sz` plus per-formula `ax1`/`ax2` yields `panel_keys == "sz"`); panel keys render in the standalone `ptr_shared_panel()`, single-formula keys render inline in that formula's controls.
findings.source: R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R shared_partition: panel_keys <- all_keys[formula_counts >= 2L]; headless ptr_shared(formulas)$panel_keys == 'sz' for two formulas sharing sz, with ax1/ax2 single-formula
findings.verification: R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:42 panel_keys<-all_keys[formula_counts>=2L]; confirmed headless ptr_shared(2 iris formulas sharing sz)$panel_keys=='sz'.

findings.claim: `ptr_signal_partial()` IS exported, so a custom placeholder's `validate_input` may legitimately call it (for mid-typing artifacts caught on the live keystroke path but not the draw path) — distinct from `rlang::abort()` for real argument/security errors.

`expr_check`'s list form is asymmetric — a bare `deny_list` REPLACES the curated 151-entry denylist with only those names (weakening protection), while `allow_list` switches to allowlist mode (`fns = setdiff(allow_list, deny_list)`) AND keeps the curated names blocked as bare data; there is no "add one name to the curated denylist" form.
findings.detail: In allowlist mode `deny_hit(name)` for bare symbols/strings checks `name %in% unsafe_expr_denylist` (the floor), while call HEADS must be in the allow set. So `system` is blocked as bare data even under an allow_list that omits it.
findings.source: R/paintr-utils.R resolve_expr_check: FALSE->off; TRUE/list-with-neither->denylist=unsafe_expr_denylist; list(deny_list)->denylist=deny_list (REPLACES); list(allow_list[,deny_list])->allowlist fns=setdiff(allow,deny)
findings.verification: R/paintr-utils.R:555-587 resolve_expr_check — FALSE->off(557); TRUE/list-neither->denylist=unsafe_expr_denylist(561,575); allow present->allowlist fns=setdiff(allow,deny)(581-583); deny only->denylist=deny_list REPLACES(586). Floor: walker deny_hit checks name %in% unsafe_expr_denylist in allowlist mode (639).

findings.claim: For a source placeholder with `shortcut = TRUE`, the sibling "type a dataset name" textInput (node$shortcut_id) is FRAMEWORK-OWNED, so the author's `build_ui` must render only node$id (or NULL for an env-name-only source) — rendering the shortcut itself would bind the id twice.
findings.source: R/paintr-registry.R source shortcut=TRUE; tests/testthat/fixtures/vignette-apps/shortcut-custom-source-env-bind/app.R build_ui returns NULL (framework-owned shortcut textInput)
findings.verification: Framework side: R/paintr-build-ui.R build_ui_for.ptr_ph_data_source (line 74) emits the shortcut textInput as STATIC UI bound at node$shortcut_id (lines 88-105, gated on isTRUE(entry$shortcut) && !is.null(node$shortcut_id)) — framework-owned, namespaced via ns_fn (header comment lines 7,17). Author side: tests/testthat/fixtures/vignette-apps/shortcut-custom-source-env-bind/app.R registers ppDataset with shortcut=TRUE and its build_ui returns NULL (lines 21-26), with the in-file comment stating the shortcut textInput is now framework-owned (build_ui_for). Claim holds; the author's build_ui renders only its own node$id (or NULL for an env-name-only source).

findings.claim: A source placeholder's default embellish_eval is an abort-guard ("`<kw>()` is only meaningful inside `ptr_app()`"), whereas value/consumer default to the identity embellish_identity() (function(x,...) x); the chosen callable is stored under the internal `runtime` record key.
findings.source: R/paintr-registry.R ptr_define_core runtime_default: source -> function(...) abort('only meaningful inside ptr_app()'); value/consumer -> function(x,...) x
findings.verification: R/paintr-registry.R ptr_define_core (lines 396-406): runtime_default for role=='source' is a closure capturing the keyword that aborts `"`<kw>()` is only meaningful inside `ptr_app()`."` (lines 397-402); for value/consumer it is embellish_identity() (line 404), which R/paintr-embellish.R:40-42 returns as `function(x, ...) x`. The user-supplied override arg was renamed embellish_eval (ADR-0027); `runtime_fn <- embellish_eval %||% runtime_default` (line 406) and stored under internal key `runtime` (line 415). Claim holds; the default-providing arg is now embellish_eval rather than `runtime`.

findings.claim: ggpaintr vignettes mark Shiny-app chunks `eval = interactive()` (runnable at the prompt, skipped on static knit / R CMD check), reserving `eval = TRUE` for pure-ggplot illustration chunks — so the vignettes knit cleanly without launching apps.
findings.source: vignettes/*.Rmd setup chunk knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval=interactive()); pure-ggplot illustration chunks override eval=TRUE
findings.verification: vignettes/{tutorial,safety}.Rmd setup chunk eval=interactive() (line 16 both); tutorial line 28 states the convention. NOTE: the two NEW vignettes contain no eval=TRUE chunk — the eval=TRUE-for-illustration half of the claim is the package convention (seen in the now-archived use-cases.Rmd `pp-toggles-naked, eval=TRUE`), not exercised by tutorial/safety themselves.
